Younglai writes: “Canadian employers added 396,800 full-time positions from October of last year, marking the strongest job growth since the turn of the century.” This is true; you have to go to March 2000, when 417,800 full-time jobs were created to find a better month by this metric. As for the headline about a new record being set for full-time jobs, that happens all the time with these data; September was a record month too. Shouldn’t the headline have been “Unemployment rate rises in October”?
